descriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Kelowna Packers 3-2 to win B.C. Junior Hockey League championship series in five games.
descriptionLong: The defending Coastal Division champion Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Interior Division champion Kelowna Packers 3-2 on March 24, 1987, at Richmond Arena, winning the best-of-nine B.C. Junior Hockey League championship series in five games. Jason Philips scored the winning goal at 9:33 of the third period, with goaltender Frank Romeo credited for a strong performance despite sinusitis. The victory extended Richmond's playoff winning streak to 13 games.
